Name: Source Note
Aidan: The passages are adapted from Nathaniel Hawthorne, The Blithedale Romance. Originally published in 1852.
Five-and-twenty years ago, at the epoch of this story, there dwelt, a man whom we shall call Fauntleroy.
column3: Five-and-twenty years ago, a man named

Aidan: A man of wealth, and magnificent tastes, and prodigal expenditure. His home might almost be styled a palace, his habits in the ordinary sense, princely.
column3: A man of wealth and magnificent tastes

Aidan: His whole being seemed to have crystallized itself into an external splendor, wherewith he glittered in the eyes of the world.
column3: His whole being seemed to have crystallized itself

Aidan: He saw himself in imminent peril of losing all that had heretofore distinguished him; and, conscious of no innate worth to fall back upon, he recoiled from this calamity, with the instinct of a soul shrinking from annihilation.
column3: He saw himself in imminent peril of losing

Aidan: Fauntleroy's guilt was discovered. He fled; his wife perished by the necessity of her innate nobleness, in its alliance with a being so ignoble; and betwixt her mother's death and her father's ignominy, his daughter was left worse than orphaned.
column3: Fauntleroy's wealth had vanished. He fled;
